name: auditize

metadata.name: auditize
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
![Auditize](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/auditize/auditize/refs/heads/master/misc/logo.svg)

# Auditize: Empower your functional logs

Auditize aims to deliver powerful audit log management capabilities to your software without the burden of developing an in-house solution.

It is a backend & frontend solution based on [Python](https://www.python.org/), [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/), [React](https://react.dev/), and [MongoDB](https://www.mongodb.com/).

Please check the [documentation](https://auditize.org) for more information about features, installation, and usage.

Auditize is licensed under the [Apache License](https://github.com/auditize/auditize/blob/master/LICENSE.txt).

classes.name: auditize.resource.pagination.cursor.service.InvalidPaginationCursor
documentation: NB: a custom exception is not really necessary, but it makes tests easier

classes.name: auditize.resource.pagination.cursor.service.PaginationCursor
documentation: This class assumes a pagination sorted by date and _id

functions.name: auditize.auth.jwt.generate_session_token_payload
documentation: NB: make this function public so we can test valid JWT tokens but signed with another key

modules.name: auditize
documentation: Shortcut for ASGI server, the entrypoint is: auditize:asgi

functions.name: auditize.log.service.consolidation._purge_orphan_consolidated_log_entity_if_needed
documentation:
This function assumes that the entity has no children and delete it if it has no associated logs.
It performs the same operation recursively on its ancestors.

methods.name: auditize.log.db._LogDbMigrator.apply_v2
documentation: NB: to avoid creating a "logs" collection with initial indexes of v1 to remove them right after
that in v2, we directly implement a v2 that will be able to manage migration from both v0 and v1

params.name: Path(description="The ID of the log to attach the file to")

Form(
            description="The 'functional' type of the attachment",
            json_schema_extra={"example": "Configuration file"},
            pattern=IDENTIFIER_PATTERN_STRING,
        )

Form(
            description="The name of the attachment. If not provided, the name of the uploaded file will be used.",
            json_schema_extra={"example": "config.json"},
        )

Form(
            description="The MIME type of the attachment. If not provided, the MIME type of the uploaded "
            "file will be used.",
            json_schema_extra={"example": "application/json"},
        )

class_variables.name: auditize.log_filter.models.LogFilter.is_favorite
documentation: Added in 0.3.0

classes.name: auditize.user.api_models.UserPasswordResetRequestRequest
documentation: NB: yes, the request of a request...

functions.name: auditize.user.service.build_document_from_user
documentation: NB: this function is let public to be used in tests when we have to inject
a user directly into database (and we want to make sure that is consistently stored)

functions.name: auditize.user.service.hash_user_password
documentation: NB: this function is let public to be used in tests and to make sure that passwords
are hashed in a consistent way

variables.name: auditize.user.service._DEFAULT_PASSWORD_RESET_TOKEN_LIFETIME
documentation: 24 hours

functions.name: auditize.helpers.datetime.serialize_datetime
documentation: Serialize a datetime object to a string in ISO 8601 format ("YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS[.sss]Z" to be specific).

functions.name: auditize.helpers.datetime.now
documentation: NB: this function doesn't do much and is mostly here to ease monkey-patching when we want
to test time-related features (e.g. token expiration)

class_variables.name: auditize.permissions.assertions.LogPermissionAssertion.permission_type
documentation: "read" or "write"

class_variables.name: auditize.permissions.assertions.EntityPermissionAssertion.permission_type
documentation: "read" or "write"

class_variables.name: auditize.permissions.assertions.EntityPermissionAssertion.entity_type
documentation: "repos", "users" or "apikeys"

variables.name: auditize.config._DEFAULT_ATTACHMENT_MAX_SIZE
documentation: 5MB

variables.name: auditize.config._DEFAULT_USER_SESSION_TOKEN_LIFETIME
documentation: 12 hours

variables.name: auditize.config._DEFAULT_ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFETIME
documentation: 10 minutes

methods.name: auditize.helpers.api.errors.ApiErrorResponse.build
documentation: NB: we use a "build" method instead of directly using the Model constructor
to better handle optional fields and subclassing
